what: Update the root `./AGENTS.md` project-instructions file so its "Repository structure", extension-list, and package-count descriptions match the current repo. Specifically: (1) In the repository-structure tree under `packages/agent-lord/extensions/`, remove the non-existent `sandbox/` entry, change `fix-ci/` to the actual thin wiring file `fix-ci.ts`, and add the two currently-undocumented wiring-file extensions `advisor.ts` (wires `advisor` tool from `@vt-pi/agent-advisor`) and `explore.ts` (wires the explore tool from `@vt-pi/agent-explorer`), keeping the directory-style extensions `command-policy/`, `git-commit/`, `no-file-writes/`, `write-guard/`. (2) Update the `packages/` section of the tree and the prose to reflect that there are nine `@vt-pi/*` packages ã `agent-advisor`, `agent-coder`, `agent-explorer`, `agent-lord`, `agent-manager`, `agent-planner`, `agent-validator`, `command-policy`, `fix-ci` ã instead of the outdated claim of "four leaf @vt-pi/* packages (command-policy, agent-advisor, agent-explorer, fix-ci)". (3) Reconcile the "How extensions are structured" / "Adding a new extension" guidance with the fact that some extensions are now single wiring `.ts` files (not `<name>/index.ts` directories) that just call a `create*Extension()` factory from a package. Do not change any code or behavior ã documentation only.
why: `./AGENTS.md` is the authoritative onboarding/instructions document for anyone (human or agent) working in this repo, but it has drifted from reality: it documents a `sandbox/` extension that no longer exists, shows `fix-ci` as a directory when it is now a `fix-ci.ts` wiring file, omits the `advisor.ts` and `explore.ts` extensions entirely, and claims there are only four `@vt-pi/*` packages when there are nine. An engineer trusting this file would look for files that don't exist, miss extensions that do, and misunderstand the package inventory and the current extension-wiring pattern. Correcting the doc restores it as a reliable map of the codebase.

tasks.title: Update AGENTS.md repository structure and package inventory to match the repo
tasks.goal: Bring the root ./AGENTS.md project-instructions file back in sync with the actual repo layout: fix the extensions tree (drop non-existent sandbox/, change fix-ci/ to fix-ci.ts, add advisor.ts and explore.ts as wiring-file extensions, keep command-policy/, git-commit/, no-file-writes/, write-guard/), update the packages listing/prose to reflect all nine @vt-pi/* packages (agent-advisor, agent-coder, agent-explorer, agent-lord, agent-manager, agent-planner, agent-validator, command-policy, fix-ci) instead of the stale 'four leaf packages' claim, and adjust the extension-structure/adding-an-extension guidance to acknowledge that some extensions are now single .ts wiring files calling a create*Extension() factory from a package.
tasks.acceptance: ./AGENTS.md no longer mentions a sandbox/ extension; the extensions tree lists advisor.ts, explore.ts, fix-ci.ts alongside the command-policy/, git-commit/, no-file-writes/, write-guard/ directories exactly as present under packages/agent-lord/extensions/; the packages/ description enumerates the nine actual @vt-pi/* packages and drops the 'four leaf @vt-pi/* packages (command-policy, agent-advisor, agent-explorer, fix-ci)' claim; and the extension-structure guidance reflects that thin wiring .ts files delegating to a package factory are a valid extension form. No source code, flake, or package files are modified.
tasks.constraints: Documentation-only change to ./AGENTS.md (the repo-root project-instructions file), not packages/agent-lord/AGENTS.md (the system prompt). Do not alter any code, build config, or behavior. Preserve the existing tone, structure, and all still-accurate content; change only what is factually out of date.
